description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped stone with a predominantly brownish hue and subtle variations in color, including hints of gray and reddish tones. The stone has a rough, natural surface texture with visible striations and minor chips, suggesting it has been weathered over time. Its form is asymmetrical, with one side appearing broader and flatter, while the opposite edge tapers to a thinner profile. The surface displays natural mottling and faint layering, typical of sedimentary rock. There are no visible artificial modifications or carvings, and the stone appears solid and dense. Such a stone could serve as a natural barrier or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A large, rough, brownish stone with an irregular, asymmetrical shape and natural surface mottling. Its weathered texture and solid form make it suitable as an urban barrier or landscape feature.
description_view_0: The stone appears broad and blocky, with a prominent rough surface and visible color variations.
description_view_1: From the side, the stone looks thin and sharply tapered, highlighting its irregular, wedge-like profile.
description_view_2: This view shows the stone's flatter, wider face with more pronounced surface mottling and subtle layering.
description_view_3: The opposite side view again reveals the stone's thin edge and uneven, jagged outline.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: Natural mottling and faint layering with irregular, non-repetitive patterns typical of sedimentary rock.
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered with minor chips and surface striations, indicating prolonged exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts moderately with manicured urban features but can blend with natural landscaping or other stones when placed in clusters.
